text.title: Flaky Test Triage Playbook Core Rules
text.summary: 7 testable rules grounding the Flaky Test Triage Playbook methodology. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; the last rule binds the decision tree skip path.

rule.statement: Each flake row scores cost = (rerun_minutes_per_week × ops_value_per_min) + (release_blocks_per_quarter × release_value). 'High/medium/low' free-form is rejected.
rule.rationale: Numeric cost-ranking is the only way to prioritise across heterogeneous flakes.

rule.statement: Each row carries disposition ∈ {fix, quarantine, delete, accept-and-budget}. Empty disposition is rejected.
rule.rationale: Indecision is the most expensive choice.

rule.statement: Quarantined tests carry an expiry date; un-quarantined tests cannot be quarantined again without a re-investigation.
rule.rationale: Quarantine is a temporary state; without expiry it becomes a graveyard.

rule.statement: Deleted tests carry a one-line justification + the surface they were covering (so coverage debt is visible).
Every row references ≥1 CI link showing the flake; verbal claims are rejected.
rule.rationale: Triage based on memory is triage based on noise.

rule.statement: Cadence is declared (weekly or biweekly); skipped triages roll the queue to the next cadence; rolling >2 cycles is an alarm.

When the decision tree routes to `skip-this-methodology`, the agent MUST emit a structured `skip` decision (with reason + suggested-sibling) rather than running Flaky Test Triage Playbook under degraded conditions.
rule.rationale: decision-tree contract. The decision tree is the only place that knows the routing; skipping must be observable so the orchestrator can log the routed-elsewhere path.
